items.label: Getting Started
items.content:
## Welcome!

**Welcome to ZYNQ!**

This library provides hundreds of games with rich metadata and thumbnails, alongside a robust WebRTC-powered real-time communication system.

Choose your preferred way to install the library below.

items.label: Script Tag
items.content:
### Script Tag

Use this for the fastest setup via CDN.

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.0.9/index.min.js"></script>
```

items.label: ES Module
items.content:
### ES Module

For modern JavaScript projects (Vite, Webpack, etc).

```javascript
import ZYNQ from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.0.9/index.min.js"
// or
import { games, peer } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.0.9/index.min.js"
```

items.label: Game Object
items.content:
## ZYNQ.games
### Game Object

A game object returned by `ZYNQ.games` methods.

```text
Game:
- name: string          // Display name of the game
- category?: string     // Optional category (e.g. "shooter", "platformer")
- alias: string         // Unique identifier used in URLs and history
- url: string           // Full playable URL
- thumb: string         // Thumbnail image URL
- devices?: number[]    // Supported device types (for filtering)

Computed properties:
- isSupported: boolean  // True if the game runs on the current device
- isFavorite: boolean   // True if the user marked it as favorite
```

children.label: list
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.list

The current filtered and/or sorted array of games. This is what you usually display in your UI.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.list
```

children.label: total
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.total

Total number of games loaded in the library.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.total
```

children.label: filteredCount
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.filteredCount

Number of games currently visible after search, filters, or sorting.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filteredCount
```

children.label: history
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.getHistory()

Returns array of recently played game aliases (max 50, persisted in localStorage).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.getHistory()
```

children.label: favorites
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.favorites

Set of favorite game aliases (persisted in localStorage).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.favorites
```

children.label: .init()
children.content:
### init(options)

Initialize the game system and load the game database. Call this first.

```javascript
await ZYNQ.games.init()
```

children.label: .search()
children.content:
### search(query)

Filter games by name or alias. Returns the `ZYNQ.games` object for chaining.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.search("alien")
```

children.label: .random()
children.content:
### random(limit = 1)

Get a random selection of games and update the filtered list.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.random(5)
```

children.label: .getRandomOne()
children.content:
### getRandomOne()

Return a single random game object (does not change the filtered list).

```javascript
const game = ZYNQ.games.getRandomOne()
```

children.label: .shuffle()
children.content:
### shuffle()

Randomly shuffle the current filtered list.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.shuffle()
```

children.label: .sortBy()
children.content:
### sortBy(key = "name")

Sort the current filtered list by a property.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.sortBy("name")
```

children.label: .sortByPopularity()
children.content:
### sortByPopularity()

Sort games by popularity score (if available in the data).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.sortByPopularity()
```

children.label: .getByAlias()
children.content:
### getByAlias(alias)

Find and return a single game by its alias.

```javascript
const game = ZYNQ.games.getByAlias("alien-hominid")
```

children.label: .getByCategory()
children.content:
### getByCategory(category)

Filter games by category and update the list.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.getByCategory("shooter")
```

children.label: .filterSupported()
children.content:
### filterSupported()

Keep only games supported on the current device.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filterSupported()
```

children.label: .filterFavorites()
children.content:
### filterFavorites()

Keep only the user's favorite games.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filterFavorites()
```

children.label: .toggleFavorite()
children.content:
### toggleFavorite(alias)

Add or remove a game from favorites (persisted automatically).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.toggleFavorite("alien-hominid")
```

children.label: .addToHistory()
children.content:
### addToHistory(alias)

Add a game alias to the play history (persisted in localStorage).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.addToHistory("alien-hominid")
```

items.label: Example
items.content:
### Games Example

```javascript
await ZYNQ.games.init()

const results = ZYNQ.games
  .search("alien")
  .filterSupported()
  .sortBy("name")
  .list

console.log(results)
```

items.label: Constructor & Singleton
items.content:
## ZYNQ.peer / ZYNQPeer

### Creating a Peer Instance

ZYNQ Peer is a clean, production-ready wrapper around **PeerJS** that unifies data channels and media streams with robust reconnection logic and race-condition protection.

Recommended ways:

- `new ZYNQ.Peer(config)` — full control
- `ZYNQPeer.get()` — singleton (recommended for most apps)

```javascript
const peer = new ZYNQ.Peer({ video: true, audio: true, txt: true })
// or
const peer = ZYNQPeer.get()
```

items.label: Options
items.content:
### Constructor Options

```javascript
{
  video: boolean,           // default: true
  audio: boolean,           // default: true
  txt: boolean,             // default: true
  debug: boolean,           // default: false
  rateLimit: number,        // messages per second, default: 30
  maxMsgSize: number,       // default: 65536
  autoReconnect: boolean,   // default: true
  maxReconnectAttempts: number, // default: 5
  rafBatching: boolean,     // default: false
  id: string | null         // custom peer ID
}
```

children.label: .connect(id)
children.content:
### connect(id)

Initiate a data connection to a remote peer. Automatically handles pending messages and reconnection.

```javascript
peer.connect("remote-peer-id")
```

children.label: .send(id, data)
children.content:
### send(id, data)

Send data to a specific peer (or "*" for all). Automatically queues and connects if needed. Rate-limited.

```javascript
peer.send("remote-peer-id", "Hello 👽")
peer.send("remote-peer-id", { type: "chat", message: "Hi!" })
peer.send("*", "broadcast message")
```

children.label: .call(id, opts)
children.content:
### call(id, options)

Start a video/audio call to a remote peer.

```javascript
peer.call("remote-peer-id", { video: true, audio: true })
```

children.label: .hangup(id)
children.content:
### hangup(id)

End an active call.

```javascript
peer.hangup("remote-peer-id")
```

children.label: .getStream(opts)
children.content:
### getStream(options)

Request local camera/microphone access. Caches the stream when constraints are unchanged.

```javascript
const stream = await peer.getStream({ video: true, audio: true })
```

children.label: .close()
children.content:
### close()

Close all active connections gracefully (data + media).

```javascript
peer.close()
```

children.label: .destroy()
children.content:
### destroy()

Fully destroy the peer, stop all streams, clear all maps and timeouts.

```javascript
peer.destroy()
```

children.label: ready
children.content:
### ready

Fired when the peer connects to the signaling server and receives its ID.

```javascript
peer.on("ready", (id) => {
  console.log("My Peer ID:", id)
})
```

children.label: open
children.content:
### open

Fired when a data connection with a remote peer is established.

```javascript
peer.on("open", (peerId) => {
  console.log("Data connection open with", peerId)
})
```

children.label: message
children.content:
### message

Fired when data is received. Payload contains `{ from, type, data }`.

```javascript
peer.on("message", (msg) => {
  console.log(`From ${msg.from}:`, msg.data)
})
```

children.label: stream
children.content:
### stream

Fired when a remote media stream arrives.

```javascript
peer.on("stream", ({ from, stream }) => {
  remoteVideo.srcObject = stream
})
```

children.label: close
children.content:
### close

Fired when a connection (data or media) is closed.

```javascript
peer.on("close", ({ id }) => {
  console.log("Connection closed with", id)
})
```

children.label: disconnect
children.content:
### disconnect

Fired when auto-reconnect is disabled and the peer disconnects.

```javascript
peer.on("disconnect", ({ id }) => { ... })
```

children.label: reconnecting
children.content:
### reconnecting

Fired during automatic reconnection attempts.

```javascript
peer.on("reconnecting", ({ id, attempt, delay }) => { ... })
```

children.label: error
children.content:
### error

Fired for any error (peer, data, media, reconnect failed).

```javascript
peer.on("error", (err) => {
  console.error(err.type, err.id, err.error)
})
```

items.label: Full Example
